what: vLLM continuous-batching + PagedAttention A/B — same H100, same Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B FP8, same 5-probe battery over real SKMD
honesty_notes: Throughput/concurrency win, not single-probe latency: one probe alone is not faster on vLLM.
Part of each probe's wall-clock is local tool execution (grep/read), which also parallelizes; vLLM batching is one contributor to the end-to-end speedup, not the sole cause.
Effective concurrency < 5 at times: the synthesized billing-regression probe does sequential authoring calls within itself.
Outputs equivalent across both runs (friction scores match within run-to-run timing noise) -> same work, ~1.77x faster.
